figures.caption: Fig. 1: Spatial and temporal factor functions ly, lz, and lt of the excitation signal in the lateral (y, z) and temporal (t) domains, adapted from [25].

Fig. 2: Sensor configurations at x = −0.1 in the y–z cross-section: uniform grid arrangement Egrid (left) and edge-only arrangement Eedge (right), showing the spatial distribution of measurement points.

Fig. 3: Exact parameter qe for a large square-shaped inclusion located in {−0.1} × [−10,−5] × [−10, 10]. The parameter takes the constant value q̃; Left: stiff inclusion with q̃ = 2.0. Right: soft inclusion with q̃ = 0.5.

Table 1: Comparison of FOM-IRGNM and trust-region (TR)-IRGNM for large inclusions. Reported are relative errors in Qh, runtimes, speed-ups, number of FOM solves, reduced dimensions (nQ, nV ), and iteration counts.
Fig. 4: Exact parameter field qe (top) and reconstructed parameter fields qTR (bottom) by TR-IRGNM for the point defect problems.
Table 2: Comparison of FOM-IRGNM and TR-IRGNM for point defect problems. Metrics are as in Table 1.
Fig. 5: Reconstructed parameter fields for different relative noise levels obtained by TR-IRGNM.
Table 3: Comparison of FOM-IRGNM and TR-IRGNM for a point defect, setup I, with varying relative noise levels δrel. ∈ {0, 10−3, 5 · 10−3, 10−2}.
Fig. 6: Objective J∗(q (i) ∗ ) plotted against total iterations (a) and against runtime (b). The colors indicate noise levels, while line styles and markers distinguish TR and FOM.

Fig. 7: (a) For the noise-free case δrel. = 0, the reduced objective Jr together with the corresponding absolute and relative errors. (b) For the noise-free case δrel. = 0, the evolution of the reduced state-space dimension dimVr over the outer iterations.

Fig. B.1: Reconstructed parameter field for the large inclusion I (stiffened case, q̃ = 2.0). Rows correspond to Experiments 1, 2(a), and 2(b), while columns show the parameter fields obtained by FOM-IRGNM, qFOM, TR-IRGNM, qTR, and their difference.

Fig. B.2: Reconstructed parameter field for the large inclusion II (softened case, q̃ = 0.5). Rows correspond to Experiments 1, 2(a), and 2(b), while columns show the parameter fields obtained by FOM-IRGNM, qFOM, TR-IRGNM, qTR, and their difference.

Fig. B.3: Reconstructed parameter field for point defect setup I. Rows correspond to Experiments 1, 2(a), and 2(b), while columns show the parameter fields obtained by FOM-IRGNM, qFOM, TR-IRGNM, qTR, and their difference.
